programme.title: NHK NEWSLINE
programme.desc: NHK NEWSLINE brings you up to date with the latest from Japan, Asia and around the world. Our team covers breaking news and major developments, with trusted anchors to tie it all together.

programme.title: CYCLE AROUND JAPAN 15min.
programme.desc: A series showcasing the stunning landscapes featured in Cycle Around Japan. This time: the beautiful islands of Yamaguchi, and the historical scenery of the Izumo Highway.

programme.title: Borderless Care
programme.desc: A new care facility in Japan without any barriers between people with disabilities and the community is spotlighted. We take a close look at how one woman user changes by interacting with the locals.

programme.desc: NHK NEWSLINE brings you up to date with the latest from Japan, Asia and around the world. Our team covers breaking news and major developments, with trusted anchors to tie it all together.

programme.title: Somewhere Street A Short Stroll
programme.desc: A 15-minute spin-off of the "Somewhere Street" series, "A Short Stroll" explores cities through traveler's eyes. Today, we visit Budapest,known as the "Peal of the Danube".

programme.title: GRAND SUMO Preview
programme.desc: NHK WORLD-JAPAN will air its Grand Sumo preview for the tourney starting May 10. Our reporter visits the city to meet wrestlers and find out their secret of winning.

programme.title: NHK NEWSLINE
programme.desc: NHK NEWSLINE brings you up to date with the latest from Japan, Asia and around the world. Our team covers breaking news and major developments, with trusted anchors to tie it all together.

programme.title: Cinematic Journey: GREAT OUTDOORS
programme.desc: Experience the wild San'in Coast. Kyle boats through Uradome's rocks, tries traditional fishing, rides fat bikes on dunes, and treks ancient lava flows.

programme.title: Asia Insight
programme.sub-title: From the Rubble to the Stage: Hualien County, Taiwan
programme.desc: The indigenous Taroko people are taking the stage to perform traditional songs and dances to entice visitors back to their famed Taroko Gorge after being impacted by a major earthquake in April 2024.

programme.title: NHK NEWSLINE
programme.desc: NHK NEWSLINE brings you up to date with the latest from Japan, Asia and around the world. Our team covers breaking news and major developments, with trusted anchors to tie it all together.

programme.title: Tokyo Cut Glass
programme.desc: Edo Kiriko cut glass techniques, passed from artisan to artisan for over 100 years. More women who now pursue the craft create popular new designs.

programme.title: Satoyama Kitchen Spring #4
programme.sub-title: Field Work Fueled by Leaf-wrapped Sushi
programme.desc: At the Satoyama Kitchen guesthouse in Hida Takayama, chef Nobuko, her cameraman husband Makoto and their cat Tama take us on a tasty journey of local cuisine served with a side of seasonal scenery.

programme.title: Direct Talk
programme.desc: The architect Sejima Kazuyo has won global attention for her delicate style, breaking from the norm to achieve free, open spaces. She explains her architectural philosophy.

programme.title: IMPACTS: Climate Change the World
programme.sub-title: COP30 in Brazil: Episode 5 Regeneration Through Agroforestry
programme.desc: Episode 5 of this series on COP30 in Brazil examines agroforestry, a combination of agriculture and forestry promoted by scientists as a potential method of regenerating rainforests.

programme.title: Ukiyoe EDO-LIFE: Niwaka Festival
programme.desc: In Yoshiwara, a high-ranking courtesan tends to a patron, while a jester attempts to entertain with an eccentric costume, hinting at the Edo townspeople's affinity for cosplay.

programme.title: Medical Frontiers
programme.desc: Hisayama Town in southern Japan has held health checkups since the 1960s. Using this data, Kyushu University identified two markers linked to early dementia. We present the latest findings.

programme.title: Spiritual Explorers
programme.sub-title: ONSEN - The Blessings of Hot Springs
programme.desc: Japan lies in a volcanic zone and boasts over 3000 onsen. We visit two old hot springs to learn the significance of bathing to the Japanese spirit.

programme.title: Time-lapse Japan
programme.desc: Experience the colors of Amami Oshima in beautiful time-lapse footage. Unique hues include "Amami Blue" ocean waters and the glossy black of silk thread that's painstakingly dyed using wood and mud.

programme.title: Cinematic Journey
programme.desc: A scientific trek into Earth's memory. See Anami's ancient strata, the singing sands of Kotobikihama, and the geological time gaps of Uradome's rocks.

programme.title: Core Kyoto
programme.sub-title: Phantom Dyeing: Ancient colors of prayer revived
programme.desc: The imperial court 1200 years ago used cloth dyed with the colors of prayer, many of which are lost to time. Some today are fascinated with these forgotten colors and are attempting to revive them.

programme.title: NHK NEWSLINE
programme.desc: NHK NEWSLINE brings you up to date with the latest from Japan, Asia and around the world. Our team covers breaking news and major developments, with trusted anchors to tie it all together.

programme.title: Century Signboards
programme.desc: Nori tsukudani, a popular topping in rice-loving Japan. Made with local nori from Tokyo Bay, it's a taste and tradition preserved for over 100 years.

programme.title: Satoyama Kitchen Spring #3
programme.desc: At the Satoyama Kitchen guesthouse in Hida Takayama, chef Nobuko, her cameraman husband Makoto and their cat Tama take us on a tasty journey of local cuisine served with a side of seasonal scenery.

programme.title: Direct Talk
programme.sub-title: Considering Our Future Through Dinosaur Research
programme.desc: In June 2025, Kobayashi Yoshitsugu published a trailblazing paper, illuminating the evolutionary process of large tyrannosaurs. He explains how such research helps us consider the future of humanity.

programme.title: DOSUKOI Sumo Salon 5 minutes
programme.sub-title: Katasukashi: The Under Shoulder Swing Down
programme.desc: This time, a technique involving clamping the opponent's shoulder then pulling them down. We take a close look and examine why the under shoulder swing down is becoming more popular!

programme.title: 1/2: People Who Don't Drink
programme.desc: 1 in 2 don't drink in Japan, where intense social drinking is normal. Through interviews of drinkers and non-drinkers we hear both sides, including a couple who went sober and opened a no-alcohol bar.

programme.title: Ukiyoe EDO-LIFE: At Roben Falls
programme.desc: We journey to a sacred mountain where pilgrims are purifying themselves in a waterfall before moving on. Some of them have even brought along offerings. And it turns out they have something to prove.

programme.title: Hometown Stories
programme.desc: Every winter, a group of performers in Kikonai, Hokkaido drench themselves in freezing water in hopes of inviting a bountiful harvest and catch. We follow efforts to preserve this timeless ritual.

programme.title: Satoyama Kitchen Diary
programme.desc: Hi! It's me, Tama. Spring's here! A season for tasty eating! But first, let me introduce my humans. Grandma Nobuko cooks and Grandpa Makoto films. Today, I hear they're preparing something...with camellias!?

programme.title: Japanology Plus
programme.desc: Sashiko is a type of traditional Japanese embroidery, once vital for the durability and warmth of clothes in cold climates. We explore how the charm of this enduring craft is being rediscovered.

programme.sub-title: Okayama: A View of the Seto Inland Sea
